report_metadata.stage: Low-grade, low mitotic index, 4cm nasal cavity

treatments.name: Endoscopic Surgical Resection

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple systematic reviews and case series confirm endoscopic resection as gold standard for sinonasal HPC/SFT; no significant difference in recurrence vs open approaches

survival_benefit.rationale: Complete excision is curative in vast majority; 5-year OS 88% and DFS 74%; 97% disease-specific survival; this is a low-grade tumor with excellent prognosis when fully excised

accessibility.rationale: Available at any otolaryngology center with endoscopic sinus surgery capability; standard surgical technique

safety_profile.rationale: Endoscopic approach is minimally invasive with low morbidity; main risk is intraoperative hemorrhage given vascular nature; lower complication rate than open approaches

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability; standard treatment regardless of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete endoscopic endonasal surgical removal of the tumor from the nasal cavity and paranasal sinuses with adequate margins. The endoscopic approach allows excellent visualization and precise dissection while preserving normal sinonasal anatomy.

key_evidence.study_name: Systematic review of treatment and prognosis of sinonasal hemangiopericytoma

treatments.name: Preoperative Embolization + Endoscopic Resection

evidence_level.rationale: Case series and systematic reviews support preoperative embolization for vascular sinonasal tumors; reduces intraoperative bleeding and facilitates resection

survival_benefit.rationale: Same curative potential as primary surgery but enables safer and more complete resection of larger vascular tumors; improves surgical field visualization

accessibility.rationale: Available at centers with interventional neuroradiology services; requires angiography and embolization expertise

safety_profile.rationale: Embolization adds small risks of stroke, cranial nerve injury, and tissue necrosis; overall low complication rate; significantly reduces intraoperative blood loss

biomarker_match.rationale: Applicable to all vascular sinonasal tumors regardless of molecular profile; particularly beneficial for larger tumors like this 4cm lesion

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Angiographic identification and selective embolization of tumor-feeding vessels (typically internal maxillary artery branches) using particles or liquid embolic agents (Onyx), performed 24-72 hours before surgery. This devascularizes the tumor, reducing intraoperative hemorrhage and enabling more complete endoscopic resection.

key_evidence.study_name: Endoscopic Resection of Sinonasal Hemangiopericytoma following Preoperative Embolisation

treatments.availability: Available at academic medical centers with interventional neuroradiology

treatments.name: Long-term Surveillance with Serial Imaging

evidence_level.rationale: Systematic reviews demonstrate 27-30% recurrence rate with up to 40% of recurrences occurring >5 years post-surgery; lifelong surveillance universally recommended

survival_benefit.rationale: Early detection of recurrence enables curative re-resection; late recurrences (>5-10 years) well documented; surveillance prevents progression to inoperable disease

accessibility.rationale: Nasal endoscopy and MRI widely available; minimal patient burden

safety_profile.rationale: Non-invasive; no toxicity; only radiation exposure from occasional CT scans

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal recommendation for all sinonasal HPC/SFT patients regardless of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Regular clinical follow-up with nasal endoscopy and periodic MRI of the paranasal sinuses to detect local recurrence. Recommended schedule: every 3-6 months for first 2 years, then annually for at least 10-15 years given the propensity for late recurrence.

key_evidence.study_name: Sinonasal Glomangiopericytoma with Prolonged Postsurgical Follow-Up

treatments.name: Adjuvant Radiation Therapy (for positive margins)

evidence_level.rationale: Retrospective data support RT for incomplete resection; systematic review shows RT advantageous when margins positive; no prospective RCTs for sinonasal HPC specifically

survival_benefit.rationale: Improves local control when margins positive (incomplete excision OR 11.5 for recurrence); not routinely needed for completely excised low-grade tumors

accessibility.rationale: Widely available radiation therapy; standard modality

safety_profile.rationale: Sinonasal radiation causes xerostomia, nasal dryness, mucosal changes, rare optic neuropathy; generally well-tolerated at doses used

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement; used based on surgical margin status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: External beam radiation therapy targeting the surgical bed to eliminate residual microscopic disease after incomplete resection. Typical doses of 50-60 Gy in conventional fractionation. Reserved for cases with positive or close margins, or recurrent disease.

key_evidence.study_name: Management of sinonasal hemangiopericytomas: a systematic review

treatments.name: Pazopanib (for recurrent/unresectable SFT)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II prospective trial specifically for SFT; first prospective SFT-specific trial; FDA approved for advanced STS

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 5.6 months in advanced typical SFT; disease control in majority of patients; reserved for unresectable/recurrent disease not applicable to this low-grade primary case

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved oral agent for STS; widely available

safety_profile.rationale: Hypertension, diarrhea, fatigue, hand-foot syndrome, hepatotoxicity; manageable but significant

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets VEGFR, PDGFR, FGFR which are downstream effectors of NAB2-STAT6 fusion-driven EGR1 activation; rational targeted approach

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral multikinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR-1/2/3, PDGFR-alpha/beta, FGFR-1/3, and c-KIT. In SFT, the NAB2-STAT6 fusion activates EGR1 which upregulates FGF2, PDGFD, and FGFR1, all direct targets of pazopanib. Inhibits tumor angiogenesis and proliferative signaling.

key_evidence.study_name: Pazopanib for treatment of typical solitary fibrous tumours: a multicentre, single-arm, phase 2 trial

treatments.name: Temozolomide + Bevacizumab (for recurrent/advanced SFT)

evidence_level.rationale: Retrospective series with 14+ patients; 79% Choi response rate; established regimen for advanced HPC/SFT; no Phase III RCT

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 9.7 months; 79% Choi response rate; superior to conventional chemotherapy for SFT; reserved for advanced disease

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA approved for other indications; widely available; oral temozolomide plus IV bevacizumab

safety_profile.rationale: Myelosuppression (main toxicity), bleeding risk from bevacizumab, fatigue, nausea; generally well-tolerated

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets vascular biology intrinsic to SFT; bevacizumab directly targets VEGF overexpressed in SFT

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Temozolomide is an oral alkylating agent that methylates DNA, causing cell death. Bevacizumab is a monoclonal antibody against VEGF-A, inhibiting angiogenesis. SFTs are highly vascular tumors, making antiangiogenic therapy rationally targeted. The combination exploits both cytotoxic and antiangiogenic mechanisms.

key_evidence.study_name: Activity of Temozolomide and Bevacizumab in the Treatment of Locally Advanced, Recurrent, and Metastatic HPC and Malignant SFT

treatments.availability: FDA Approved (both agents for other indications); off-label for SFT

treatments.name: Axitinib (for progressive SFT)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II investigator-driven trial; ORR 41% by Choi criteria; promising in progressive SFT

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS >12 months in responding patients; active in malignant SFT variant; reserved for progressive disease

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for renal cell carcinoma; available off-label; oral administration

safety_profile.rationale: Hypertension, diarrhea, fatigue, hand-foot syndrome, thyroid dysfunction; similar profile to other VEGFR TKIs

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets VEGFR pathway highly relevant to SFT vascular biology

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Selective oral inhibitor of VEGFR-1/2/3 with higher potency than pazopanib for VEGFR. Provides potent antiangiogenic activity against SFT's intrinsically vascular tumor biology.

key_evidence.study_name: Activity of axitinib in progressive advanced solitary fibrous tumour: Phase 2 study

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for RCC; off-label for SFT

treatments.name: Sunitinib (for progressive SFT)

evidence_level.rationale: Case series and retrospective data; confirmed activity in SFT with long-lasting responses; no prospective SFT-specific trial

survival_benefit.rationale: Long-lasting non-dimensional responses reported; disease stabilization in majority; reserved for progressive disease

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for GIST and RCC; widely available oral agent; off-label for SFT

safety_profile.rationale: Fatigue, diarrhea, hand-foot syndrome, hypertension, myelosuppression, cardiac toxicity; requires monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets PDGFRB and VEGFR, both relevant to SFT biology driven by NAB2-STAT6 fusion

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral multikinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR-1/2/3, PDGFR-alpha/beta, KIT, FLT3, and RET. Provides broad antiangiogenic and antiproliferative activity against the vascular SFT tumor type. PDGFRB is a downstream target of the NAB2-STAT6 fusion.

key_evidence.study_name: Sunitinib malate in solitary fibrous tumor (SFT)

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for GIST/RCC; off-label for SFT

treatments.name: Regorafenib (for progressive SFT)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II exploratory study; 43% Choi ORR; activity confirmed in SFT; highest preclinical activity (95% tumor volume inhibition)

survival_benefit.rationale: 43% Choi response rate; shorter median PFS than pazopanib/axitinib possibly due to dose reductions; preclinical models show strongest activity

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for CRC, GIST, HCC; available off-label; oral administration

safety_profile.rationale: Hand-foot syndrome, fatigue, diarrhea, hypertension, hepatotoxicity; high dose reduction rate limits tolerability

biomarker_match.rationale: Broad multikinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR, PDGFR, FGFR, and other kinases relevant to SFT

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Oral multikinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR-1/2/3, PDGFR, FGFR, KIT, RET, RAF, and TIE2. Provides the broadest kinase inhibition profile among antiangiogenic agents, showing 95% tumor volume inhibition in SFT xenograft models. Targets multiple downstream effectors of NAB2-STAT6.

key_evidence.study_name: Regorafenib in advanced solitary fibrous tumor: Phase II exploratory study

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for CRC/GIST/HCC; off-label for SFT

treatments.name: Doxorubicin-based Chemotherapy (for advanced SFT)

evidence_level.rationale: Standard first-line chemotherapy for advanced STS per NCCN; Phase III data in STS; limited SFT-specific prospective data

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 10-20% in SFT with doxorubicin alone; inferior to antiangiogenic approaches for SFT specifically; reserved for advanced/metastatic disease

accessibility.rationale: Widely available standard chemotherapy; FDA approved for STS

safety_profile.rationale: Cardiomyopathy, myelosuppression, nausea, alopecia; cumulative dose-limiting cardiotoxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: Non-specific cytotoxic mechanism; not targeted to SFT biology; inferior to antiangiogenic agents for this tumor type

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Doxorubicin intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II, causing DNA strand breaks and apoptosis in rapidly dividing cells. Standard first-line chemotherapy for advanced soft tissue sarcomas, though SFT responds less favorably than to antiangiogenic agents.

key_evidence.study_name: The role of chemotherapy in advanced solitary fibrous tumors: a retrospective analysis

treatments.name: Mithramycin A (EGR1-DNA Interaction Disruptor)

evidence_level.rationale: Preclinical data showing disruption of NAB2-STAT6 condensates and reversal of aberrant gene expression; no clinical trial for SFT

survival_benefit.rationale: Preclinical mechanism proof-of-concept; dissolves NAB2-STAT6 fusion-driven nuclear condensates; clinical benefit unknown

accessibility.rationale: Research compound; not FDA approved; significant hepatotoxicity has limited clinical development historically

safety_profile.rationale: Historical use limited by hepatotoxicity, myelosuppression, hemorrhagic syndrome; narrow therapeutic window

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets EGR1-DNA interactions, the primary downstream effector of NAB2-STAT6 fusion; mechanistically ideal for SFT

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Mithramycin A disrupts EGR1-DNA interactions by binding to GC-rich DNA sequences. Since the NAB2-STAT6 fusion drives SFT pathogenesis through EGR1 activation, mithramycin dissolves NAB2-STAT6 nuclear condensates formed by liquid-liquid phase separation, reversing aberrant gene expression and chromatin binding.

key_evidence.study_name: NAB2-STAT6 Fusion Proteins Drive Nuclear Condensate Formation in SFT

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (for recurrent SFT)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial completed; one partial response in anaplastic SFT; failed to improve PFS-6 overall; limited evidence

survival_benefit.rationale: Failed primary endpoint of PFS-6 improvement; one partial response in anaplastic SFT subtype; not recommended for typical/low-grade SFT

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for multiple indications; widely available; off-label use possible

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized immune-related adverse event profile; generally well-tolerated

biomarker_match.rationale: SFT typically has low mutational burden and limited immune infiltration; immunotherapy rationale weak for this tumor type

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody that blocks the PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint, restoring T-cell mediated antitumor immunity. Limited activity in SFT likely due to low tumor mutational burden and immunologically 'cold' tumor microenvironment.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II, open-label, single-arm trial of pembrolizumab for recurrent meningioma and solitary fibrous tumor

clinical_trials.title: Pazopanib for Advanced Typical Solitary Fibrous Tumours: Phase 2 Multicentre Trial
clinical_trials.early_results: Median PFS 5.6 months; 51% response rate by Choi criteria; manageable toxicity; suggests first-line role for advanced SFT

clinical_trials.title: Axitinib in Progressive Advanced Solitary Fibrous Tumour: Phase 2 Study
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 41% by Choi criteria; >12 month PFS in half of malignant SFT patients

clinical_trials.title: Regorafenib in Advanced Solitary Fibrous Tumor: Phase II Exploratory Study
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 43% by Choi criteria; high dose reduction rate

clinical_trials.title: Pembrolizumab for Recurrent Meningioma and Solitary Fibrous Tumor
clinical_trials.early_results: One partial response in anaplastic SFT; failed primary PFS-6 endpoint

clinical_trials.title: Temozolomide + Bevacizumab in Advanced Hemangiopericytoma/SFT
clinical_trials.early_results: 79% Choi response rate; median PFS 9.7 months in prior series

combination_strategies.rationale: Embolization devascularizes the highly vascular tumor 24-72 hours before surgery, reducing intraoperative hemorrhage and enabling more controlled, complete endoscopic resection of 4cm nasal cavity tumors

RT recommended when surgical margins are positive or close; incomplete excision has 11.5x odds ratio for recurrence; RT improves local control especially in locations where re-excision is anatomically limited

Synergistic combination exploiting SFT's vascular nature; temozolomide provides cytotoxic DNA damage while bevacizumab targets VEGF-driven angiogenesis; 79% Choi response rate superior to either agent alone
Antiangiogenic agents may normalize tumor vasculature and enhance immune cell infiltration; combining antiangiogenic with checkpoint blockade may overcome SFT's immunologically cold microenvironment

supportive_care.evidence: Systematic review of 337 sinonasal glomangiopericytoma cases; up to 40% of recurrences occur >5 years post-surgery
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of recurrence enables curative re-resection; prevents progression to locally advanced or metastatic disease; recurrence is curable if detected early

supportive_care.approach: Nasal Saline Irrigation and Mucosal Care
supportive_care.evidence: Post-endoscopic sinus surgery standard of care; reduces crusting and synechia formation
supportive_care.benefit: Maintains nasal patency after surgery; reduces crusting; prevents synechiae that could obscure surveillance endoscopy; improves patient comfort and quality of life

supportive_care.approach: Psychological Support for Rare Tumor Diagnosis
supportive_care.evidence: ASCO supportive care guidelines; rare disease patient support literature
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses anxiety from rare tumor diagnosis and surveillance burden; provides coping strategies for lifelong follow-up requirement; connects patients with rare tumor support networks

supportive_care.approach: Multidisciplinary Skull Base Tumor Board Review
supportive_care.evidence: NCCN and ESMO guidelines for sinonasal tumors; improves surgical planning and treatment decisions
supportive_care.benefit: Optimizes surgical approach (endoscopic vs combined); determines need for preoperative embolization; ensures appropriate pathology review with STAT6 immunohistochemistry and molecular confirmation
